Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results explicitly state that DUF288 contains At2g41770 and At3g57420, which were named STL1 and STL2, respectively; thus STL1 = At2g41770.

Applied_Species.reason: The study is performed in Arabidopsis; the title and Results repeatedly refer to Arabidopsis plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Introduction explicitly mentions Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species.reason: STL1 is one of two Arabidopsis proteins identified in the DUF288 family and experimentally studied in Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper identifies the system as Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Figures and methods use Col-0 as the wild-type/control background.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the experimental Arabidopsis background, but the paper does not explicitly state STL1 was sourced from a Col-0 accession rather than simply amplified from an Arabidopsis seedling cDNA library.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: STL1 is described as a DUF288 protein with a region homologous to CAZy GT75 proteins; hydrophobicity analysis suggests a single transmembrane domain and type II topology, and localization is Golgi.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results show STL1 is Golgi-localized and protease protection/GO-PROMTO assays indicate a type II topology with the C-terminus in the Golgi lumen.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The core phenotype directly concerns reduced cellulose production/content in stl1stl2 mutants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cellulose is a polysaccharide; this is standard and directly consistent with the paper's focus on cellulose as a wall polymer.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results document defects in primary wall cellulose, secondary wall cellulose-rich stems/fibres/xylem, and seed mucilage cellulose rays/adherent mucilage.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links cellulose to plant growth, cell elongation, secondary wall integrity, and seed mucilage architecture through the observed mutant phenotypes.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show that loss of STL1 together with STL2 reduces cellulose content and impairs CSC Golgi distribution, secretion, motility/activity, and assembly.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state microarray data suggested STL1 and STL2 have similar expression profiles and are active in expanding cells or cells producing secondary cell walls; pSTL1:STL1-GFP confirmed this.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The expression pattern described above is directly shown in the Arabidopsis experimental system.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly used pSTL1:STL1-GFP and pUb10:STL1-mCherry constructs; these were used for complementation/localization.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used in the paper for STL1-related conclusions: T-DNA double mutants, pSTL1:STL1-GFP complementation, Saeman hydrolysis, solid-state NMR, X-ray diffraction, localization, split-ubiquitin, BiFC, FRAP, and BN-PAGE.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Split-ubiquitin assays showed interaction of STL1 with CesA1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8 and STL2; BiFC confirmed interactions with primary wall CesAs and STL proteins. The full list is supported at least by split-ubiquitin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary matches the Results: redundant function with STL2, reduced cellulose (~50% hypocotyls, ~40% stems) in double mutants, CesA interaction/co-localization, CSC assembly/secretion/motility defects, and DXD motif point mutations impairing function.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states single mutants had little/no obvious growth phenotype, only the C-terminal construct complemented, DD205-206AA and DVD297-299AVA failed to complement while R271A and DD591-592AA did, and matrix polysaccharides were not substantially altered.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Etiolated hypocotyl assays and treatments with isoxaben and DCB are explicitly described and used phenotypically.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential application to modify biomass/cell wall traits is a plausible extrapolation, but the paper does not directly test or state breeding/engineering utility.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The reported double-mutant phenotypes include short roots/hypocotyls, stunted growth, hypersensitivity to isoxaben/DCB, reduced secondary wall thickness, and seed mucilage defects.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray co-expression analysis is supported, and a solid-state NMR data repository is provided, but calling the latter 'omics data' is imprecise since NMR is not an omics dataset.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results explicitly identify At3g57420 as STL2.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments are conducted in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper refers to Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species.reason: STL2 is one of the two Arabidopsis DUF288 proteins directly identified and tested.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study organism is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the wild-type background used throughout.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: As with STL1, Col-0 is the experimental background, but the exact source accession for the cloned STL2 ORF is not explicitly stated beyond Arabidopsis seedling cDNA.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: STL2 is a DUF288 protein, homologous in part to CAZy GT75 proteins, and the STL proteins are described as single-pass type II Golgi membrane proteins.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: STL2-GFP localizes to Golgi compartments and GO-PROMTO data support type II topology with the catalytic/C-terminal region in the Golgi lumen.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: STL2 is directly linked through double-mutant analysis to cellulose production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The double-mutant phenotypes involve primary wall, secondary wall, and seed mucilage cellulose-associated structures.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The mutant phenotypes support roles of cellulose in growth, elongation, secondary wall integrity, and seed mucilage structure.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support that STL2 functions redundantly with STL1 in cellulose production and CSC assembly/trafficking-related processes.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state STL1 and STL2 have similar microarray expression profiles and are active in expanding cells and cells producing secondary walls.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The same Arabidopsis expression profile is the one described in the paper.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly generated and used a pUb10:STL2-GFP construct for localization and functional testing.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all used to support STL2-associated conclusions, largely through the stl1stl2 mutant and STL2-GFP localization/complementation plus interaction and CSC assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Split-ubiquitin assays support interactions of STL2 with STL1 and CesA1/3/4/6/7/8; BiFC confirms interactions with primary-wall CesAs and STL proteins.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary accurately reflects the Results for STL2: redundancy with STL1, cellulose-deficient double-mutant phenotypes, Golgi localization/co-localization with CesAs, interaction with CesAs, and contribution to CSC assembly/secretion/activity.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper shows single stl2 mutants lacked obvious growth defects and that STL1 and STL2 can interact with each other; however, explicit STL2 homodimerization is less direct in text but referred to for STL proteins generally.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Etiolated hypocotyl growth and isoxaben/DCB treatments are explicitly part of the experimental design.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application not directly tested or stated in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper clearly shows severe developmental and cellulose-related defects in the combined loss of STL2 with STL1.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray co-expression data are supported, but describing the NMR data repository as 'omics data' is not directly faithful to the paper.
